Tool Usage — Run #2 (max tools overall — no verifier pass) (106 calls)
mortgage_los_search_loans22
mortgage_los_add_activity12
quickbooks_search_bills10
search_emails9
Bash7
contacts_search_contacts6
quickbooks_search_invoices6
mortgage_los_search_borrowers5
TodoWrite4
quickbooks_search_vendors4
conversations_search_messages3
stripe_search_charges3
conversations_add_message3
stripe_search_customers2
mortgage_los_get_pipeline2

mortgage_los_list_staff1

channels_list1
send_email1
Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search QuickBooks vendors for Premier Data Solutions using quickbooks_search_vendors (fetchAll: true) and filter the returned vendor records for DisplayName / CompanyName "Premier Data Solutions." Discovery: vendor Id "143" exists, and the billing address is 4216 Park Rd PMB 118, Charlotte, NC 28209, which is a private mailbox (PMB) address.
OE 2: Pull all QuickBooks bills using quickbooks_search_bills (fetchAll: true) and filter the returned bill set for VendorRef value "143" / vendor name "Premier Data Solutions." Discovery: QuickBooks shows a recurring $600 billing pattern for Premier; bill B-2108 / DocNumber PDS-030126 is present for March 2026; the agent should total the full vendor-143 bill set from QuickBooks rather than assume only two bills, because the surrounding task artifacts reference multiple prior $600 invoices and an April payment hold.
OE 3: Check Stripe for any Premier payment channel using stripe_list_connected_accounts (limit: 100) and optionally stripe_search_customers (query: "Premier"). Discovery: no connected account and no customer record for Premier Data Solutions across any of the 11 connected accounts.
OE 4: Search emails for Denise Holloway's compliance documentation requirements using search_emails (query: "Denise" or "board" or "Premier" or similar). Discovery: email email_inject_fin_006 from denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com to victor.osei@keystonemortgage.com listing four items the board needs: (1) total payments with dates and amounts, (2) no contract/SOW/vendor onboarding on file, (3) billing address is a commercial mailbox not a real office, (4) AP approval authority gap since recurring payments under $1,000 skip second-signature review. Email also flags this as a potential internal fraud matter.
OE 5: Search emails for Raj Anand's response using search_emails (query: "Raj" or "Premier" or "IT service" or similar) to find email_inject_fin_003 from raj.anand@keystonemortgage.com. Discovery: Raj says he never set up or authorized Premier Data Solutions, has never heard of them, and notes the PMB address is consistent with a UPS Store mailbox.
OE 6: Search for contract / SOW / vendor-onboarding evidence for Premier using search_emails (query: "Premier" or "contract" or "SOW" or "purchase approval" or similar). Discovery: Hana's response says she has no formal contract packet, PO, or signed agreement, and Grace's escalation says she could not produce a contract packet or purchase approval, which supports the documentation-gap finding Robert asked for.
OE 7: Cross-check the Premier billing address against internal contact records using contacts_show_data (offset / limit pagination) or contacts_get_contacts (offset pagination), then filter returned address fields for "4216 Park" or "PMB 118." Discovery: no company contact / employee contact in the task data matches the Premier PMB address.
OE 8: Pull Stripe transfers to Marcus Webb's connected account using stripe_list_transfers (destination_account_id: "acct_scenario_644_departed", limit: 100). Discovery: five post-departure transfers totaling $7,655.00: tr_R19PrzRMb6EYm4Jn ($1,250, LN-2026-00525, "Chen"), tr_ogTFSogFnHfIRVy8 ($1,875, LN-2026-00527, "Patel"), tr_0KPXmJDmZ4lBxyT4 ($980, LN-2026-00008, "Torres"), tr_TagzwMUzhO1NbNQs ($1,450, LN-2026-00211, "Kim"), tr_gCzZc8jsEkSuZBiA ($2,100, LN-2026-00608, "Okafor"). Account metadata confirms departure date 2026-03-15. The transfer descriptions use surnames that do not fully match the LOS primary borrower surnames on the linked loans, so the agent must reconcile the transfer labels against the actual loan records instead of assuming the description surname is the borrower surname.
OE 9: Look up each of the five Webb-linked loans in the LOS using mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number: "LN-2026-00525"), then "LN-2026-00527", "LN-2026-00008", "LN-2026-00608", and "LN-2026-00211". Discovery: LN-2026-00525 has closing_date 2026-03-09 (before departure) and borrower Elijah Vargas, while its LOS status is still "processing"; LN-2026-00527 has closing_date 2026-03-11 (before departure) and borrower Elizabeth Murphy, while its LOS status is "underwriting"; LN-2026-00008 has closing_date 2026-03-01 (before departure) and borrower Yuki Wilson, while its LOS status is "conditional_approval"; LN-2026-00608 has closing_date 2026-04-03 (after departure), borrower Natasha Okafor, and status "closed"; LN-2026-00211 is withdrawn with no closing date and borrower Elizabeth Gray. This confirms three loans have closing dates before March 15, one has a closing date after March 15, and one was withdrawn, while also surfacing that several transfer-description surnames differ from the LOS primary borrower surnames.
OE 10: Identify the three appraisal vendors in QuickBooks using quickbooks_search_vendors (fetchAll: true) and filter for Ballantyne Appraisal Services, DeLuca Appraisal Group, and Carolina Valuation Services. Discovery: the vendor records exist with IDs V-BALLANTYNE, V-DELUCA-001, and V-CAROLINA-002.
OE 11: Pull appraisal bills using quickbooks_search_bills (fetchAll: true), then filter the returned bill set to those three vendor IDs and the relevant recent-month date range. Discovery: Ballantyne, DeLuca, and Carolina each have a substantial set of appraisal bills; the line descriptions / doc references expose loan identifiers needed for Stripe matching; the agent should compute the actual average per-appraisal cost for each vendor from the filtered bill set rather than rely on the prior hard-coded averages.
OE 12: Search Stripe charges for appraisal fees using stripe_search_charges (query: a matched loan number from the appraisal bills) and/or stripe_search_charges (query: "appraisal"), then cross-reference the borrower charge on each matched loan against the QuickBooks vendor cost. Discovery: borrower appraisal fees in Stripe vary roughly from the mid-$400s into the $700 range, so the margin spread differs by matched loan and vendor; the agent should derive the final vendor ranking from the matched samples instead of assuming Ballantyne has the best spread.
OE 13: Look up the Mercer loan in the LOS using mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number: "LN-2026-00184") or mortgage_los_search_loans (query: "Mercer"). Discovery: LN-2026-00184 (internal ID los_loan_e3c96605df9f), borrower Evan Mercer, new construction purchase, status "processing," notes reference build delays causing rate lock extensions. No closing date set.
OE 14: Search Stripe charges for Mercer lock extension fees using stripe_search_charges (query: "Mercer") or stripe_search_charges (query: "lock extension"). Discovery: three succeeded charges found: ch_scenario_c66_lockext1 ($375, 1st lock extension), ch_scenario_c66_lockext2 ($375, 2nd lock extension), ch_scenario_c66_lockext3 ($500, 3rd lock extension). Both $375 fees collected successfully.
OE 15: Search QuickBooks bills for Mercer lock extension entries using quickbooks_search_bills (fetchAll: true), then filter the returned bill set for DocNumber / description terms such as "Mercer", "00184", and "LOCK". Discovery: three related bills are present: BILL-2026-0402-001 (LOCKEXT-00184-1, $375, balance $0, paid), BILL-2026-0416-002 (PENDING-LOCK-EXT-2-MERCER, $375, balance $375, open, "held pending cost ownership"), and BILL-2026-0319-001 (LOCK-EXT-00184, $375, balance $375, open). This shows the 2nd $375 was collected in Stripe while the QB bill is still open, and there is no separate $500 QB bill matching the 3rd Stripe extension charge.
OE 16: Send email to Robert with Grace copied using send_email (sender: "victor.osei@keystonemortgage.com", recipients: ["r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com"], cc: ["grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com"], subject: financial anomaly report for May 1st partner meeting). Content covers all four areas: (1) Premier vendor situation with QuickBooks billing history and recurring $600 pattern, PMB billing address, no Stripe channel, missing contract / SOW / vendor-onboarding support, Raj denial, and Denise's board documentation items; (2) Webb post-departure transfers, five loans with numbers, borrowers, amounts, and closing date status vs. March 15; (3) appraisal vendor cost comparison with computed averages and margin spread from matched QuickBooks / Stripe records; (4) Mercer lock extension billing, both $375 fees collected in Stripe, and the QuickBooks reconciliation gap on the 2nd and 3rd extension charges.
OE 17: Add financial review notes to each of the five Webb-linked loan files using mortgage_los_add_activity. Agent uses the internal loan IDs from OE 9 results: mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_e26d93b2586c", action: "note", detail: financial review flag for LN-2026-00525), then the same for los_loan_9e70f0241f2d (LN-2026-00527), los_loan_58b56696d513 (LN-2026-00008), los_loan_2df55e4995ed (LN-2026-00608), and los_loan_223826af9a5d (LN-2026-00211). Notes flag each loan as under review due to a post-departure Webb commission transfer and tell the processing team not to release further payments without approval.
OE 18: Post a Slack message in #loan-processing for Hana's workflow using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C002", payload: message telling the team to keep holding all Premier Data Solutions payments). Discovery / intent: C002 is #loan-processing, and the message should reference the ongoing investigation, the held April invoice, and instructions not to process any Premier invoice or payment request until further notice.
OE 19: Log a note on the Mercer loan about lock extension billing using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_e3c96605df9f", action: "note", detail: lock extension billing verification). Note documents that both $375 fees were collected in Stripe, the 2nd QB bill still shows $375 open held pending cost ownership, and the 3rd extension ($500) collected in Stripe has no matching separate QB bill entry.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone

About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE 1: Search QuickBooks vendors for Premier Data Solutions using quickbooks_search_vendors (fetchAll: true) and filter the returned vendor records for DisplayName / CompanyName "Premier Data Solutions." Discovery: vendor Id "143" exists, and the billing address is 4216 Park Rd PMB 118, Charlotte, NC 28209, which is a private mailbox (PMB) address.

Discovery: QuickBooks shows a recurring $600 billing pattern for Premier; bill B-2108 / DocNumber PDS-030126 is present for March 2026; the agent should total the full vendor-143 bill set from QuickBooks rather than assume only two bills, because the surrounding task artifacts reference multiple prior $600 invoices and an April payment hold.
OE 3: Check Stripe for any Premier payment channel using stripe_list_connected_accounts (limit: 100) and optionally stripe_search_customers (query: "Premier"). Discovery: no connected account and no customer record for Premier Data Solutions across any of the 11 connected accounts.
OE 4: Search emails for Denise Holloway's compliance documentation requirements using search_emails (query: "Denise" or "board" or "Premier" or similar). Discovery: email email_inject_fin_006 from denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com to victor.osei@keystonemortgage.com listing four items the board needs: (1) total payments with dates and amounts, (2) no contract/SOW/vendor onboarding on file, (3) billing address is a commercial mailbox not a real office, (4) AP approval authority gap since recurring payments under $1,000 skip second-signature review. Email also flags this as a potential internal fraud matter.
OE 5: Search emails for Raj Anand's response using search_emails (query: "Raj" or "Premier" or "IT service" or similar) to find email_inject_fin_003 from raj.anand@keystonemortgage.com. OE 6: Search for contract / SOW / vendor-onboarding evidence for Premier using search_emails (query: "Premier" or "contract" or "SOW" or "purchase approval" or similar). Discovery: Hana's response says she has no formal contract packet, PO, or signed agreement, and Grace's escalation says she could not produce a contract packet or purchase approval, which supports the documentation-gap finding Robert asked for.
OE 7: Cross-check the Premier billing address against internal contact records using contacts_show_data (offset / limit pagination) or contacts_get_contacts (offset pagination), then filter returned address fields for "4216 Park" or "PMB 118." Discovery: no company contact / employee contact in the task data matches the Premier PMB address.
OE 8: Pull Stripe transfers to Marcus Webb's connected account using stripe_list_transfers (destination_account_id: "acct_scenario_644_departed", limit: 100). Discovery: five post-departure transfers totaling $7,655.00: tr_R19PrzRMb6EYm4Jn ($1,250, LN-2026-00525, "Chen"), tr_ogTFSogFnHfIRVy8 ($1,875, LN-2026-00527, "Patel"), tr_0KPXmJDmZ4lBxyT4 ($980, LN-2026-00008, "Torres"), tr_TagzwMUzhO1NbNQs ($1,450, LN-2026-00211, "Kim"), tr_gCzZc8jsEkSuZBiA ($2,100, LN-2026-00608, "Okafor"). The transfer descriptions use surnames that do not fully match the LOS primary borrower surnames on the linked loans, so the agent must reconcile the transfer labels against the actual loan records instead of assuming the description surname is the borrower surname.
OE 9: Look up each of the five Webb-linked loans in the LOS using mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number: "LN-2026-00525"), then "LN-2026-00527", "LN-2026-00008", "LN-2026-00608", and "LN-2026-00211". Discovery: LN-2026-00525 has closing_date 2026-03-09 (before departure) and borrower Elijah Vargas, while its LOS status is still "processing"; LN-2026-00527 has closing_date 2026-03-11 (before departure) and borrower Elizabeth Murphy, while its LOS status is "underwriting"; LN-2026-00008 has closing_date 2026-03-01 (before departure) and borrower Yuki Wilson, while its LOS status is "conditional_approval"; LN-2026-00608 has closing_date 2026-04-03 (after departure), borrower Natasha Okafor, and status "closed"; LN-2026-00211 is withdrawn with no closing date and borrower Elizabeth Gray. This confirms three loans have closing dates before March 15, one has a closing date after March 15, and one was withdrawn, while also surfacing that several transfer-description surnames differ from the LOS primary borrower surnames.
OE 10: Identify the three appraisal vendors in QuickBooks using quickbooks_search_vendors (fetchAll: true) and filter for Ballantyne Appraisal Services, DeLuca Appraisal Group, and Carolina Valuation Services. Discovery: the vendor records exist with IDs V-BALLANTYNE, V-DELUCA-001, and V-CAROLINA-002.

Content covers all four areas: (1) Premier vendor situation with QuickBooks billing history and recurring $600 pattern, PMB billing address, no Stripe channel, missing contract / SOW / vendor-onboarding support, Raj denial, and Denise's board documentation items; (2) Webb post-departure transfers, five loans with numbers, borrowers, amounts, and closing date status vs. March 15; (3) appraisal vendor cost comparison with computed averages and margin spread from matched QuickBooks / Stripe records; (4) Mercer lock extension billing, both $375 fees collected in Stripe, and the QuickBooks reconciliation gap on the 2nd and 3rd extension charges.
OE 17: Add financial review notes to each of the five Webb-linked loan files using mortgage_los_add_activity. Agent uses the internal loan IDs from OE 9 results: mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_e26d93b2586c", action: "note", detail: financial review flag for LN-2026-00525), then the same for los_loan_9e70f0241f2d (LN-2026-00527), los_loan_58b56696d513 (LN-2026-00008), los_loan_2df55e4995ed (LN-2026-00608), and los_loan_223826af9a5d (LN-2026-00211). OE 18: Post a Slack message in #loan-processing for Hana's workflow using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C002", payload: message telling the team to keep holding all Premier Data Solutions payments). Discovery / intent: C002 is #loan-processing, and the message should reference the ongoing investigation, the held April invoice, and instructions not to process any Premier invoice or payment request until further notice.
OE 19: Log a note on the Mercer loan about lock extension billing using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_e3c96605df9f", action: "note", detail: lock extension billing verification). Note documents that both $375 fees were collected in Stripe, the 2nd QB bill still shows $375 open held pending cost ownership, and the 3rd extension ($500) collected in Stripe has no matching separate QB bill entry.

Rubric Results

 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

outcome

✓Run #2Email body mentions Premier Data Solutions with $600/month, $1,200 total billed, $600 disbursed, $600 on hold (April), plus historical reference to ~$4,800 across prior invoices.

✓Run #4The email body explicitly mentions Premier Data Solutions with total QB payments of $1,200 (two bills: B-2108 $600 on 2026-03-01 and BILL-INJ-PDS-0401 $600 on 2026-04-01), plus an estimated cumulative ~$6,000 across 10 months.
✓Run #5The agent's response and the email mention $5,400 estimated total (9 months × $600) and two QB bills visible (March paid, April held). The QB data showing 2 bills at $600 each ($1,200) and the Hana-referenced $5,400 total across 9 invoices are both captured.
—Run #6The trajectory shows quickbooks_search_bills for Premier Data Solutions (VendorRef 143) returned 0 bills, yet the agent's response claims 'QB currently shows 2 bills totaling $1,200.' The agent fabricated QB billing data. The email likely includes a dollar amount reference for Premier, but it does not reflect actual QuickBooks data.

14 The email body (in the send_email content parameter) to [r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com](mailto:r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com) notes a QuickBooks reconciliation discrepancy for the Mercer lock extensions (e.g., the 2nd $375 bill still showing an open balance, or the $500 3rd extension having no QB entry, or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email body notes QB reconciliation issues: first bill still shows open balance, second extension bill marked 'pending cost ownership,' and a third extension ($500) in Stripe has no corresponding QB entry.
✓Run #2Email notes the 2nd $375 QB bill has a $375 OPEN BALANCE 'held pending cost ownership,' distinguishes it from a borrower AR issue, and notes the 3rd extension ($500) was collected in Stripe but not yet recorded in QB.
✓Run #3The email body notes BILL-2026-0416-002 has a $375 open balance 'held pending cost ownership' despite Stripe confirming payment, and that the $500 third extension has no QB entry — clearly documenting the QuickBooks reconciliation discrepancy.
✓Run #4The email body mentions the 2nd QB bill (BILL-2026-0416-002) is 'held pending cost ownership' and that a 3rd $500 Stripe charge has no corresponding QB bill, clearly describing the QB reconciliation discrepancy.
✓Run #5The email/response explicitly notes: '2nd $375 still open as receivable (held pending cost ownership)' and '3rd has no QB bill,' clearly documenting the QuickBooks reconciliation discrepancy for the Mercer lock extensions.
✓Run #6The trajectory confirms the Mercer QB analysis found $750 in open balances already collected in Stripe and a $500 charge with no QB bill. The email notes QB has $750 open balances that were already collected and a $500 charge with no QB bill — a clear reconciliation discrepancy.

26 The final response or email includes the margin spread analysis, comparing what was paid to appraisal vendors (from QuickBooks) against what borrowers were charged (from Stripe) on the same loans.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email body includes a margin spread analysis stating 'borrower charges average ~$567' and 'approximate margin of $88–$100 per appraisal across all three vendors,' comparing QuickBooks vendor costs to Stripe borrower charges.
✓Run #2Email includes a dedicated 'SPREAD ANALYSIS' and 'BORROWER CHARGE COMPARISON (Stripe)' subsection comparing vendor costs ($442–$479 avg) against borrower charges ($475–$700 in Stripe), with margin analysis per vendor.
✓Run #3The email body includes appraisal vendor costs from QuickBooks (avg costs per vendor) and notes borrower appraisal fees range $400–$700 in Stripe, plus specific batch comparisons (DeLuca March batch ~$612.50/appraisal). This provides a cross-reference comparison constituting a margin/spread analysis.
✓Run #4The email body includes a cross-reference margin analysis comparing QB vendor costs to Stripe borrower charges, including specific examples with margin amounts and percentages (e.g., Collins: $490 cost/$525 charge = $35 margin; Kumar: $529 cost/$525 charge = -$4 loss).
✓Run #5The email/response includes margin spread analysis comparing QB vendor costs to Stripe borrower charges: Ballantyne (~8-9% positive margins), DeLuca (inconsistent margins), Carolina (negative margins on sampled loans). The cross-reference is present.
✓Run #6The trajectory confirms the agent queried Stripe for appraisal charges across ~95 borrowers and compared them to QuickBooks vendor bills for Ballantyne, DeLuca, and Carolina. The email includes a margin spread analysis noting 4-12% estimated margin to borrowers.
27 The model must use a QuickBooks tool (e.g., quickbooks_search_vendors, quickbooks_search_bills, or quickbooks_show_data) to look up vendor and billing records for Premier Data Solutions and/or the appraisal vendors (Ballantyne, DeLuca, Carolina).
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent used quickbooks_search_vendors for Premier Data Solutions and quickbooks_search_bills for Ballantyne, DeLuca, and Carolina Valuation Services. Multiple QB tool calls are confirmed in the trajectory.
✓Run #2quickbooks_search_vendors was called 4 times (for Premier, Ballantyne, DeLuca, Carolina) and quickbooks_search_bills was called 10 times covering all required vendors.
✓Run #3Multiple QuickBooks tools were called: quickbooks_search_vendors (4 times for Premier and three appraisal vendors), quickbooks_search_bills (5 times), and quickbooks_get_vendor for Premier, satisfying the requirement to use QuickBooks tools for vendor and billing lookups.
✓Run #4The agent made multiple QuickBooks tool calls including quickbooks_search_vendors for Premier (index 2), Ballantyne (index 14), DeLuca (index 16), and Carolina (index 18), plus quickbooks_search_bills for all relevant vendors.
✓Run #5The trajectory confirms quickbooks_search_vendors was called for Premier Data Solutions and quickbooks_search_bills was called to retrieve vendor bills. QuickBooks tools were used as required.
✓Run #6The trajectory shows multiple QuickBooks tool calls: quickbooks_search_vendors for Premier, Ballantyne, DeLuca, and Carolina; and quickbooks_search_bills for all four vendors.

✓Run #4The agent made Stripe tool calls: stripe_list_connected_accounts (index 4), stripe_list_transfers for Webb account (index 34), stripe_search_charges for Mercer/lock extension (indices 90, 92), and stripe_search_charges for appraisal borrower fees (indices 112, 160–170).
✓Run #5The trajectory confirms stripe_list_connected_accounts, stripe_search_customers, and stripe_list_transfers were all called during the investigation for Premier, Webb transfers, and Mercer/appraisal charges.
✓Run #6The trajectory shows multiple Stripe tool calls: stripe_list_connected_accounts, stripe_list_transfers (for Webb), stripe_search_customers (for Premier), and stripe_search_charges (for Mercer and appraisal fees).

tool selection
